segments.text: Hello, welcome to this session about
Gemini Cloud
Assist. My name is Leanit. I'm a
developer relations engineer at Google
Cloud. Today I'm going to review the new
capabilities of Gemini Cloud Assist that
were announced during Cloud Next. Let me
start with a short overview of what is
new and then move to realtime
demonstration of the new products. Some
of the products that you will see are in
preview but available to all Google
Cloud
users. I encourage you to try them in
your respective
environments. Google Cloud manages
resources and projects. Any resource
that you create like a virtual machine
or a service account belongs to a single
project. This traditional resource
centric approach introduces a large
amount of complexity to application
design, deployment and management. It is
especially hard when applications cross
project boundaries.
This forces application and platform
developers to translate application
design into low-level infrastructure
architectures. Operators are faced with
additional challenges when they need to
troubleshoot application functions going
through the maze of cloud
infrastructures in search for the
application's footprint. Other tasks
such as capacity planning or cost
optimization also require continuous
translation between application business
model and cloud resources. Recognizing
these challenges, Google has been
placing AI at the center of your Google
Cloud
experience. By today, you have been
seeing Gemini across multiple areas of
Google Cloud. To improve application
management and operations, Google Cloud
leverages Gemini's state-of-the-art
models to help you perform application
life cycle
tasks. This assistance is focused on
three
areas. Design
operations and optimization of your
applications. I'll demonstrate how
Gemini helps to manage applications
related tasks in the demo. Let's zoom in
into these three areas of the
application life cycle. When doing
design, we need to answer many
questions. What is the right services
required to run the
application? How should the services
interact between themselves to follow
the recommended architectural patterns
to do it in a secure way? Moving to
operations, one of the critical tasks is
to find the quickest way to mitigate the
failure. answer questions like is it a
problem in my application or is it an
infrastructure problem and similarly
when the application is operational
reduce the ways to optimize capacity
management or track and reduce
operational costs in each area. Gemini
Cloud Assist provides you with tools and
best practices to complete your tasks.
Gemini Cloud Assist helps to utilize
best practices when designing your
application, providing you with
Terraform scripts for deploying your
application and identifying a specific
component that experience a problem.
Using Gemini Cloud Assist, you can see
the usage of your resources and control
operational costs in an application
centric view. This is the vision of
AIdriven application management. How
does Gemini cloud assist address all
these questions? The cloud assist stack
look like this. At the base level, it
has a collection of information
providers. They are integrations or what
we call agents that use Google services
such as resource manager, asset
inventory, and cloud monitoring to
retrieve information about your
application and its infrastructure. The
next level is an application hub, a
platform for managing workloads and
resources in an application ccentric
way. We will see some functions of the
application hub in the demo. On top of
app, there are new products including
cloud hub, investigations, application
design center and other that support
users operations in application match.
They help to design, operate, and
optimize workloads, resources, and
assets of your application. With the
help of Gemini AI
models, developers and platform
engineers no longer need to search for

applications across projects and cloud
console
views. Using these products, they access

Assist has a holistic view based on

upto-date information retrieval on one

widgets from different devices on
another. After this introduction, let's
see how this new Gemini Cloud Assist
